rulings.Case Title: UNIFUND CCR PARTNERS, A NEW YORK PARTNERSHIP VS. GUAN SHAO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Order To Show Cause, Etc.
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 28, 2024 line 1. ASSIGNEE NDS, LLC'S NOTICE OF HEARING ON ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE, ETC. is GRANTED. No opposition filed. Moving party to prepare a submit an Order to Show Cause for execution on February 28, 2024. The hearing date on the Order to Show Cause shall be March 29, 2024. Moving party shall personally serve the order no later than March 6, 2024 and the proof of service to be filed no later than March 8, 2024. Judgment debtor shall file a response no later than March 18, 2024. Moving party shall file a reply, if any, no later than March 22, 2024. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 1466 CHESTNUT STREET LLC, A NEVADA LIMITED VS. ANN GERVASI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Summons; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Kurt Van Sciver
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 28, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANT NATALIE ZIVNUSKA'S MOTION TO QUASH SUMMONS is GRANTED. Plaintiff failed to cite any authority for the proposition that 5 days summons are appropriate under the circumstances of this case asserting separate causes of action for ejectment, waste, declaratory relied and nuisance. If Plaintiff wishes to proceed with this action inclusive of all these causes of action Plaintiff shall obtain 30 days summons and this case will be placed on general civil track. This action cannot proceed partially on an unlawful detainer track (5-day notice motions and 20-day notice UD trial) and partially on a general civil track (handled via case management and subject to civil case procedural rules). If Plaintiff wishes to proceed only on the UD cause of action, Plaintiff shall dismiss other causes of action and re-serve the operative summons, complaint along with a filed copy of the request for dismissal. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: POLK STREET ASSOCIATES VS. BERBER GROUP, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Lift The Stay Of This Action; Declaration Of Jon H. Freis
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 28, 2024 line 4. PLAINTIFF POLK STREET ASSOCIATES' MOTION TO LIFT THE STAY OF THIS ACTION is GRANTED. Prematurely filed Second Amended Complaint is stricken (thus, March 6, 2024 motions are taken off calendar). Demurrer to the First Amended Complaint having been previously sustained, there is currently no operative complaint on file. Plaintiff shall file and service a Second Amended Complaint within 5 days of notice of entry of this order. No Second Amended Complaint shall be submitted for filing until an Order granting this motion is executed, filed and served. The Court notes that there would have been no confusion about the Court's ruling on the Demurrer to the First Amended Complaint had Plaintiff waited until a formal order was entered thereon. Request for sanctions is denied. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
